Alerts & notifications
Critical — act now
DATP Public Comment reading room is live. The City of Colorado Springs Digital Accessibility Transition Plan (Draft 2026), with the full plan embedded as a PDF and twelve structured public comments residents can adapt and submit. Comment portal links live in the reading room.
Public comment · COS Digital Accessibility Transition Plan · May 2026
Open the DATP reading room →
BOCC petition — service plan transparency & algorithmic pricing disclosure. A general resident petition with four narrow asks within existing BOCC authority. Sign and send.
Civic action · El Paso County BOCC · May 2026
Open petition letter →
Veterans' petition to the EPC BOCC. Companion petition for veterans of any branch — service plan transparency and algorithmic pricing protections, framed around military housing harms.
Civic action · Veterans · El Paso County BOCC · May 2026
Open veterans' petition →
LDC update — public comment on service plan transparency. A 300-word comment ready to submit through the El Paso County Land Development Code update process.
Public comment · LDC update · El Paso County · May 2026
Open LDC comment →
Background brief — algorithmic rent pricing in El Paso County. Sourced reference for journalists, policy analysts, and the COS Chamber housing committee: federal antitrust action, local policy options, and the case for BOCC-level service plan transparency.
Reference · Housing & pricing · El Paso County BOCC · May 2026
Read background brief →
HOME-ARP comment window is live — closes May 26, 2026. COS is collecting public comment on the HOME-ARP Allocation Plan amendment via an ArcGIS Survey123 form Esri's own ACR rates as "Partially Supports" on WCAG 2.1 A/AA. A WCAG 2.1 AA Drupal Webform replacement is ready to install — bilingual EN/ES, JSON webhook + staff email + native storage, inline accommodation intake.
24 CFR 91.105 · ADA Title II · May 4, 2026
Open the accessible HTML form preview → Open the HOME-ARP bundle README →
Downtown Funding and Records Search Search BID assessments, DDA grants, TIF reimbursements, board meetings, and financial records in one place.
Annual adopted budget — C.R.S. 29-1-101 et seq · April 10, 2026
Open the search portal →
ADA Title II deadline: April 24, 2026 — 16 days. All local government websites must meet WCAG 2.1 AA. Zero district sites are confirmed compliant on PDF documents.
HB21-1110 · 28 C.F.R. Part 35 · April 8, 2026
Open district audit dashboard →
249 of 284 metro districts have no website. CORA request is the only way a resident can access their own district's records.
EPC Assessor audit · April 2026
See district audit →
WS Districts (WSDM) serves 30+ districts on a single WordPress site. Last calendar entry: 2021. No governance docs. No financials. One compliance letter fixes all 30+ simultaneously.
Platform audit · April 2026
Draft compliance letter →
High priority
All posted PDFs likely fail WCAG 1.3.1. Meeting minutes, budgets, and audit docs are almost certainly untagged. This is the real HB21-1110 failure — not the homepage.
ADA document audit · April 2026
View ADA analysis →
0 of 8 audited sites post their service plan. C.R.S. §32-1-202 requires it. This is the foundational legal document. Not one district links it publicly.
Governance gap · §32-1-202
See audit dashboard →
Annual audit documents: publicly posted by 0 of 8 districts. Required by §32-1-106. Due to State Auditor by July 31. The clock is running.
Governance gap · §32-1-106 · due July 31
See audit dashboard →
Informational
2 CORA requests pending response. Military housing numbers and district governance records both awaiting reply. Response window is still open.
CORA tracker · April 2026
View tracker →
Wolfersberger compliance and Bellamese analysis complete. New findings ready for review on developer agreements, compliance history, and parcel-level records.
Compliance analysis · April 2026
Read analysis →
Jurisdiction finder is live. Enter any El Paso County address to find your metro districts, school district, city jurisdiction, and utilities — plain language, no login.
New · April 2026
Find my jurisdiction →
CSPD Blotter — Weekly History is live. 271 incidents pulled directly from the live blotter feed, paired with a Poisson 95% confidence band to flag statistical anomalies week to week. Tabs are full APG-pattern keyboard accessible; every chart has an aria-label.
New · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
Open blotter dashboard →
Lorson Ranch financial dashboard is live. Seven cross-pledged metro districts, $94.2M aggregate audited deficit, IGA money flows, and a per-home tax calculator — sourced from 2026 budgets and 2024 audits. Companion plain-language briefing available as a download.
New · Special Districts · April 2026
Open Lorson Ranch dashboard →
CBI Missing Persons form — accessible rebuild is live. Resolves all seven critical/serious findings from the ADA audit: skip link, live regions, focus management, EN/ES localization, real-time validation messaging. Drops into the existing Drupal Webform infrastructure with optional Claude API routing.
New · State · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
Open CBI form rebuild →
LexisNexis ADAM Program dashboard — accessible rebuild is live. Audit and reference rebuild of the missing-children dashboard: APG-pattern tabs with roving tabindex and full keyboard nav, solid amber focus rings (3:1 against any surface), darker success-green for AA contrast, and "View Poster" placeholders converted from dead anchors to real buttons.
New · Private Sector · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
Open ADAM dashboard →

Government structure · El Paso County, Colorado

How government is organized here — and where to find the work

Federal sets the floor. State writes the rules. Local makes the day-to-day decisions about your address. Every box below is a real agency with real decision-making authority; every link inside a box opens a record, audit, dashboard, or form that lives on this site.

If you're looking for something specific, the Start Here block on the homepage will route by ZIP. This page is for understanding the structure.

Tier 3 · Federal & private sector

Federal government & private-sector accountability

The federal layer sets minimum standards (ADA Title II, antitrust, HUD funding) and operates national reporting infrastructure. Private-sector platforms appear here when their products are operated at federal scale and their accessibility or competition behavior affects residents nationwide.

U.S. Department of Justice — Civil Rights

Active

Enforces ADA Title II across local government websites and services. Filing path for accessibility complaints; co-plaintiff with state AGs on antitrust matters (RealPage).

Work on this site

  • DOJ Civil Rights complaint — accessible redesign →

Federal Trade Commission (FTC)

Active

Consumer protection and fraud reporting; ReportFraud.ftc.gov is the national consumer-fraud complaint portal.

Work on this site

  • ReportFraud.ftc.gov — WCAG 2.1 AA audit →

Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI)

Active

Federal law-enforcement intake — tip submission, IC3 cybercrime, public-records requests.

Work on this site

  • FBI tip-submission — redesign proposal →

U.S. Dept of Housing & Urban Development (HUD)

Active

Funds local housing programs (HOME-ARP, CDBG). Requires public-comment processes under 24 CFR 91.105 — which is what drove the accessible HOME-ARP form rebuild.

Work on this site

  • HOME-ARP form — accessible HTML preview →
  • HOME-ARP bundle — Drupal Webform replacement →

LexisNexis Risk Solutions — ADAM Program

Private sector

Private-sector missing-children dashboard, used by law enforcement agencies nationally. Listed here because the tool is public-facing and accessibility matters for grieving families and tip-submitters.

Work on this site

  • ADAM Program — accessible rebuild & audit →
Federal sets the floor ↓ State writes Colorado rules

Tier 2 · State of Colorado

State of Colorado

Statewide agencies whose authority reaches into El Paso County: the Attorney General's enforcement surface, the Civil Rights Division's parallel ADA filing path, the Bureau of Investigation's missing-persons system, the Department of Local Affairs (which holds the special-district registry under CRS Title 32), and the Department of Public Health & Environment.

Colorado Attorney General (COAG)

Active

State enforcement for consumer protection, antitrust, civil rights. Filed alongside DOJ in the RealPage algorithmic-pricing antitrust case.

Work on this site

  • COAG complaint form — accessible →
  • File a complaint — redesign →
  • Compliance brief →
  • Accessibility tool comparison →

Colorado Civil Rights Division (CCRD)

Active

State-level civil-rights and ADA enforcement. Parallel filing path to DOJ Civil Rights — file with one, both, or escalate from one to the other.

Work on this site

  • CCRD complaint form — accessible →

Colorado Bureau of Investigation (CBI)

Active

State law-enforcement intelligence and missing-persons system. Public-facing intake forms must be accessible to families in crisis.

Work on this site

  • CBI Missing Persons form — accessible rebuild →

Dept of Local Affairs (DOLA)

Active

Holds the statewide special-district registry under CRS Title 32. When a local metro district hides its records, DOLA is a parallel filing surface.

Work on this site

  • DOLA CORA — audit & redesign →

Colorado Dept of Public Health & Environment (CDPHE)

Active

State public-health and environmental data. Holds mortality, environmental-health, and vital-statistics records for El Paso County.

Work on this site

  • El Paso County Mortality Dashboard →
  • CDPHE CoHID — ADA audit →

Office of the Governor

Reference

Executive branch. Sets statewide policy priorities; signs the bills that DOLA, CCRD, CDPHE, and others implement. No accountability work hosted here yet.

  • No site work yet — reference only
State writes Colorado rules ↓ Local touches your address

Tier 1 · Where you live

Local — El Paso County & Colorado Springs

Five sub-groups: the County itself, the City of Colorado Springs, downtown taxing districts (BID/DDA/TIF), the 284 special districts billed to your property tax, and the regional bodies (PPROEM emergency operations) that span jurisdiction lines.

El Paso County

Board of County Commissioners (BOCC)

Active

5-member elected board. Approves special-district service plans, sets the property-tax mill levy, drives the Land Development Code update process. The actionable lever for special-district transparency.

Work on this site

  • Resident petition — service plan transparency →
  • Veterans' petition to the BOCC →
  • LDC update — public comment →
  • Algorithmic rent pricing — background brief →

El Paso County Sheriff

Active

County law enforcement: unincorporated areas, courts, jail. Crime data, public-safety records.

Work on this site

  • El Paso County Public Safety Dashboard →

County Assessor

Active

Real-property valuation and the ownership record. The first place to look when a metro district has no website but is taxing you anyway.

Work on this site

  • Assessor — property conveyances →

Planning & Community Development

Active

Land Development Code (LDC), zoning, subdivisions, population data. Where service plans for new special districts are reviewed and approved.

Work on this site

  • LDC — contact & navigation →
  • LDC — accessibility statement →
  • Population components dashboard →
  • Compared to what — dashboard →

Public Works — Road & Bridge

Active

County roads, bridges, infrastructure. Their public website triggered a manual ADA audit and a deployable WordPress mu-plugin patch.

Work on this site

  • Road & Bridge — ADA audit & remediation bundle →

El Paso County Citizen Connect

Active

County's resident portal — submit issues, request services. Audited for WCAG 2.1 AA with a redesign proposal.

Work on this site

  • Citizen Connect — ADA audit →

Colorado Springs Utilities (CSU)

Active

Municipal enterprise — water, electric, gas, wastewater for Colorado Springs and surrounding areas. CORA-responsive on residential account data.

Work on this site

  • CSU Residential Water Accounts Dashboard →

City of Colorado Springs

Mayor & City Council

Reference

Elected city government. Approves budget, sets ordinances, oversees departments. No accountability work hosted on this site yet.

  • No site work yet — reference only

Housing & Community Vitality

Active

Administers HUD HOME-ARP funds, runs the public-comment process under 24 CFR 91.105. The HOME-ARP form replacement and the DATP reading room originate here.

Work on this site

  • HOME-ARP — accessible HTML form preview →
  • HOME-ARP — Drupal Webform bundle README →

Office of Accessibility (DATP)

Active

Implements the city's Digital Accessibility Transition Plan. Active public-comment window with a structured reading-room view of the plan.

Work on this site

  • DATP — open reading room & comments →

Colorado Springs Police Dept (CSPD)

Active

City law enforcement. Public-records, blotter feed, field-entry forms, and neighborhood-level data infrastructure.

Work on this site

  • CSPD blotter — weekly history dashboard →
  • CSPD audit — BJPT-TKZQ →
  • CSPD five questions →
  • Fatality register — BJPT-TKZQ →
  • Audit memo — 12C consolidated →
  • CSPD records page — redesign →
  • CSPD field-entry form →
  • CSPD field-entry prototype →
  • COS records portal — redesign →

City Clerk / Public Records (CORA)

Active

Colorado Open Records Act (CORA) intake. The accessible CORA form and implementation guide live here.

Work on this site

  • COS CORA — accessible request form →
  • COS CORA — implementation guide →

Traffic Engineering & Safety

Active

Signal timing, crash data, FHWA funding, red-light camera program, Safety Action Plan.

Work on this site

  • COS Traffic Safety hub →
  • Signal-timing action tool →
  • Red-light camera data →
  • SAP — public comments →
  • SAP — context & analysis →

goGov / City Permits, Licenses & Records

Active

Plain-language contact portal — 119 topics across 13 groups, scenario journeys ("building a home," "opening a restaurant"), jurisdiction badges. WCAG 2.1 AA.

Work on this site

  • City permits, licenses & records — portal →

Forestry

Active

City tree inventory and urban-forest data.

Work on this site

  • Trees of Colorado Springs →

Community Resources

Active

Helping Hands — El Paso County resource finder, food/housing/health/legal aid directory.

Work on this site

  • Helping Hands — resource finder →

Downtown taxing & business districts

Downtown Funding & Records

Active

Business Improvement District (BID), Downtown Development Authority (DDA), Tax-Increment Financing (TIF) zones. Aggregated assessments, grants, reimbursements, board meetings, and financial records.

Work on this site

  • Downtown funding & records — search portal →
  • Downtown business directory →

Special districts — 284 in El Paso County

Metro districts — audit & toolkit

Active

Cross-cutting analysis: 249 of 284 districts have no website. 0 of 8 audited post their service plan. CORA is the only path to records. Governance gap by §32-1-202 and §32-1-106.

Work on this site

  • EPC metro-district audit dashboard →
  • Metro-district citizen toolkit →
  • Wolfersberger compliance · Bellamese analysis →

Lorson Ranch Metropolitan Districts 1–7

Active

Seven cross-pledged metro districts financing one community. $94.2M aggregate audited deficit, IGA money flows, per-home tax calculator.

Work on this site

  • Lorson Ranch — financial dashboard →

Teller 911

Active

911 emergency-services special district straddling Teller and El Paso counties. CORA project documenting dispatcher staffing and call-volume realities.

Work on this site

  • Teller 911 — Unsung Heroes CORA project →

Regional & cross-jurisdictional

Pikes Peak Regional Emergency Operations (PPROEM)

Active

Joint EPC + COS all-hazards emergency-management body. Adopted regional EOP covers 14 sections, all 28 ESFs, hazard risk by jurisdiction. Operates the live regional alerts feed.

Work on this site

  • Pikes Peak Regional EOP — full document →
  • PPROEM live regional alerts →

Find My Jurisdiction

Active

Cross-jurisdiction address resolver. Enter a ZIP or address; get the city, county, school district, metro districts, and utilities that touch it.

Work on this site

  • Find my jurisdiction →

CORA Requests Tracker

Active

Open CORA filings, response windows, and Fort Carson military-housing numbers. Cross-agency.

Work on this site

  • CORA tracker · military numbers →
↑ Back to top

Get email updates

We'll email you a plain-language summary with links to the source documents whenever this office posts new public-record material.

By subscribing you agree to receive emails about this topic. We'll send a confirmation link first. Unsubscribe any time. (consent v2026-04-30-v1)
